pages.text: Storage Units add $(thing)type capacity$(): room for more distinct item identities. They do not cap the quantity stored for a type that already fits.

A plain item, a damaged item, and versions with different enchantments or other properties can use separate type slots. If an existing type already has a slot, adding more of that exact type does not consume another slot.

Each Storage Unit $(thing)drops itself$() at the same tier. When one is removed, the network's $(thing)available type capacity decreases$(), but $(thing)stored items stay in the Core$(). Add capacity before inserting a brand-new type.

The ornament shows progression: T1 has a copper-bound cell; T2 iron braces; T3 a gold and lapis lattice; T4 a diamond and quartz cross; T5 a prismarine and ender halo; T6 a netherite and amethyst crown. T4 is the first Nether upgrade; T4-T6 are optional capacity and prestige.
See $(l:magic_storage:recipes)Recipe Catalog$() for the exact crafting layout.
See $(l:magic_storage:recipes)Recipe Catalog$() for the exact crafting layout.
